records.ety: [OE. fair, fayer, fager, AS. fæger; akin to OS. & OHG. fagar, Icel. fagr, Sw. fager, Dan. faver, Goth. fagrs fit, also to E. fay, G. fügen, to fit. fegen to sweep, cleanse, and prob. also to E. fang, peace, pact, Cf. Fang, Fain, Fay to fit.]

defs: Free from spots, specks, dirt, or imperfection; unblemished; clean; pure.
Pleasing to the eye; handsome; beautiful.
quotes: Who can not see many a fair French city, for one fair French made.

defs: Without a dark hue; light; clear; as, a fair skin.
quotes: The northern people large and fair-complexioned.

defs: Not overcast; cloudless; clear; pleasant; propitious; favorable; -- said of the sky, weather, or wind, etc.; as, a fair sky; a fair day.
quotes: You wish fair winds may waft him over.

defs: Free from obstacles or hindrances; unobstructed; unincumbered; open; direct; -- said of a road, passage, etc.; as, a fair mark; in fair sight; a fair view.
quotes: The caliphs obtained a mighty empire, which was in a fair way to have enlarged.

defs: Without sudden change of direction or curvature; smooth; flowing; -- said of the figure of a vessel, and of surfaces, water lines, and other lines.

Characterized by frankness, honesty, impartiality, or candor; open; upright; free from suspicion or bias; equitable; just; -- said of persons, character, or conduct; as, a fair man; fair dealing; a fair statement.
Pleasing; favorable; inspiring hope and confidence; -- said of words, promises, etc.
quotes: When fair words and good counsel will not prevail on us, we must be frighted into our duty.

defs: Distinct; legible; as, fair handwriting.
Free from any marked characteristic; average; middling; as, a fair specimen.
quotes: The news is very fair and good, my lord.

defs: Clearly; openly; frankly; civilly; honestly; favorably; auspiciously; agreeably.
Fairness, beauty.
A fair woman; a sweetheart.
quotes: I have found out a gift for my fair.

defs: Good fortune; good luck.
To make fair or beautiful.
To make smooth and flowing, as a vessel's lines.

records.ety: [OE. feire, OF. feire, F. foire, fr. L. fariae, pl., days of rest, holidays, festivals, akin to festus festal. See Feast.]

defs: A gathering of buyers and sellers, assembled at a particular place with their merchandise at a stated or regular season, or by special appointment, for trade.
A festival, and sale of fancy articles. erc., usually for some charitable object; as, a Grand Army fair; a church fair.
A competitive exhibition of wares, farm products, etc., not primarily for purposes of sale; as, the Mechanics' fair; an agricultural fair.

an exhibition by a number of organizations, including governmental organizations, for the purpose of acquainting people with such organizations or their members, not primarily for commercial purposes; as, the 1939 World's Fair.
quotes: Meet me in St. Louis, Louis Meet me at the fair Don't tell me the lights are shining Anyplace but there.
